reason: SAD.md demonstrates complete FR coverage (FR-01 through FR-108 all traceable to specific modules), coherent 5-layer architecture with explicit downward-only dependency flow and no circular dependencies, valid hub module contracts for all 5 directories with cohesion ratios stated, all 10 high-risk modules identified in the Architecture Risk Registry with correct NP-07/NP-13/NP-15 forced patterns, all 9 error levels L0-L9 mapped to concrete SRS failure modes, all 38 NFRs traced to modules, and technology stack fully aligned with SRS constraints (Python 3.11, FastAPI, PostgreSQL 16+pgvector HNSW m=16/ef=64, Redis 7, OpenAI text-embedding-3-small 1536-dim, gpt-4o primary with gemini-1.5-flash fallback). The SAB block is structurally valid with all required fields. No critical or high gaps identified.

gaps.message: app.infra.deployment.py is listed as a module handling FR-95‚Äì98 (Docker Compose, K8s HPA/PDB, backup, rollback), but deployment artifacts are typically not runtime Python modules ‚Äî the boundary between code module and infrastructure config is not clarified; this could cause confusion during implementation

admin/reports.py is designated the hub for the admin directory but its sibling coverage ratio (‚â•0.38) is the lowest of all hubs; with only 4 siblings (rbac, gdpr, webui, odd_sql) this is technically achievable but the document does not confirm all 4 call log_admin_action() or build_report() per function body ‚Äî worth verifying during implementation

FR-106 (k6 load tests: smoke/load/stress/spike) is traced to tests.load which is outside the app/ directory tree; the SAD module structure does not include a tests/ directory layout, leaving the k6 test organization implicit
